title: Executive Order 10521—Administration of Scientific Research by Agencies of the Federal Government

flags.justification: NSF's role is explicitly to 'recommend,' 'study,' and review 'in concert with each' agency, a coordination and advisory role rather than direct command authority over other agencies' research programs.
Sec. 9 requires reports to the President as required.

Multiple related coordination functions (policy recommendations, program review, equipment-sharing, classification standards) all coherently serve one purpose: federal scientific research coordination.
Coordination and advisory functions with no binding compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination framework for an ongoing government function, evergreen by nature.

Sec. 5 explicitly requires the Foundation to study effects on educational institutions 'while safeguarding the strength and independence of the Nation's institutions of learning' — a genuinely protective, academic-freedom-conscious provision.
Sec. 5's explicit consultation-with-universities requirement provides real external stakeholder input, though no formal compliance regime exists.

A foundational order establishing NSF's coordinating role over the entire federal scientific research enterprise at the dawn of the Cold War science-funding buildup, a lasting institutional structure that the later post-Sputnik science policy apparatus would build directly upon.

Sec. 4 explicitly states that other agencies' mission-related basic research 'is recognized as important and desirable... and shall continue' — explicitly protecting rather than centralizing other agencies' research prerogatives.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. A foundational document for the modern federal science-policy coordination apparatus, scoring low due to its careful, protective, coordination-focused (rather than command-focused) design — including an explicit safeguard for university independence.
